positions.title: The Role of the Unconscious
positions.text_evidence: Freud posits that the unconscious mind plays a crucial role in shaping human behavior and experiences. It contains repressed memories and desires that influence our thoughts and actions, often without our conscious awareness.

positions.title: Dreams as Wish Fulfillment
positions.text_evidence: Freud argues that dreams serve as a form of wish fulfillment, allowing individuals to explore desires and conflicts that are not acceptable in waking life. This perspective highlights the significance of dreams in understanding the psyche.

positions.title: Civilization's Impact on Mental Health
positions.text_evidence: In 'Civilization and Its Discontents,' Freud discusses how the demands of civilization can lead to neuroses and dissatisfaction. He suggests that the restrictions imposed by societal norms often conflict with innate human desires.

positions.title: Sexuality's Role in Hysteria
positions.text_evidence: We are only able to produce very incomplete evidence in favour of our view that sexuality seems to play a principal part in the pathogenesis of hysteria as a source of psychical traumas and as a motive for ‘defence’ - that is, for repressing ideas from consciousness.

positions.title: Confidentiality in Clinical Practice
positions.text_evidence: It would be a grave breach of confidence to publish material of this kind, with the risk of the patients being recognized and their acquaintances becoming informed of facts which were confided only to the physician.

positions.title: Divergence in Interpretations
positions.text_evidence: If at some points divergent and indeed contradictory opinions are expressed, this is not to be regarded as evidence of any fluctuation in our views. It arises from the natural and justifiable differences between the opinions of two observers who are agreed upon the facts and their basic reading of them.

positions.title: Value of Initial Views
positions.text_evidence: Even to-day I regard them not as errors but as valuable first approximations to knowledge which could only be fully acquired after long and continuous efforts.

positions.title: Importance of Psychosexual Factors
positions.text_evidence: The attentive reader will be able to detect in the present book the germs of all that has since been added to the theory of catharsis: for instance, the part played by psychosexual factors and infantilism.

positions.title: Role of Dreams in Psychoanalysis
positions.text_evidence: The importance of dreams and of unconscious symbolism.
